widgets_filter_rs.purpose: Nucleo-powered fuzzy filter state and matching logic

key_structures.name: FilterState
key_structures.responsibility: Holds mutable filter query and reusable nucleo matcher; implements matches() to test cards against pattern

key_structures.name: FilterMatch
key_structures.responsibility: Encapsulates match result with highlight indices into card title portion

widgets_filter_rs.algorithm: Combined haystack '{id} {title}' matched via nucleo::Pattern::indices(); converted indices from combined space to title-only space via offset calculation

changes: Added filter: Option<String> field — persisted filter query
Added filter_state: Option<FilterState> field — mutable state during filter mode
Added enter_filter_mode() — initializes FilterState and switches to Mode::Filter
Added confirm_filter() — persists query to filter field, returns to Normal
Added clear_filter() — clears both filter and filter_state, returns to Normal
Added apply_filter_collapse() — recalculates column collapse based on match results
Wired handle_filter() function for Mode::Filter branch
Backspace pops from query, updates filter field, calls apply_filter_collapse()
Char handler appends to query, updates filter field, calls apply_filter_collapse()
Added KeyCode::Char('/') in handle_normal to enter filter mode
card_to_list_item() now accepts optional filter_match: Option<&FilterMatch>
Title rendering logic modified to apply yellow+bold styling to matched indices
Integrated highlight rendering into card spans construction

patterns_discovered.description: Match against '{id} {title}' concatenation to allow flexible filtering by either card ID or title; requires index offset conversion post-match
patterns_discovered.benefit: Users can filter by card ID or partial title; single pattern search over both fields

patterns_discovered.description: Nucleo indices in combined haystack must be offset-adjusted to title-only space via: title_offset = id_char_len + 1, then filter indices >= title_offset and subtract offset
patterns_discovered.complexity: Requires careful char count calculation (not byte count) and deduplication of sorted indices

patterns_discovered.description: filter: Option<String> persists across mode transitions; filter_state: Option<FilterState> is ephemeral and dropped when filter is cleared or confirmed
patterns_discovered.rationale: Allows kanban widget to continue applying confirmed filter while filter_state is unavailable for edits in Normal mode

patterns_discovered.description: apply_filter_collapse() called on every keystroke to check all cards against current pattern; columns with zero matches are immediately collapsed
patterns_discovered.ux_impact: Users see live feedback as they type; visual feedback of matching card count per column

patterns_discovered.description: Pattern::new() uses CaseMatching::Smart, Normalization::Smart, AtomKind::Fuzzy for intelligent fuzzy matching
patterns_discovered.robustness: Smart case and normalization handle accents, case-insensitivity, and Unicode automatically

gotchas_discovered.context: Converting combined haystack indices to title-only indices requires char count, not byte count
gotchas_discovered.solution: Used card_id.chars().count() to get proper char offset; nucleo indices are char-based, not byte-based
gotchas_discovered.impact: Would have caused off-by-one highlight errors if using byte offsets on multi-byte UTF-8 strings

gotchas_discovered.context: Indices from nucleo may contain duplicates or be unsorted; filtering by title_offset and then deduping is necessary
gotchas_discovered.solution: Sort unstable, then dedup; filter indices >= title_offset and subtract offset
gotchas_discovered.impact: Ensures clean, sorted highlight indices for rendering without duplicate style spans

gotchas_discovered.context: Allocating a new Matcher on every keystroke would be expensive
gotchas_discovered.solution: FilterState holds a single reusable Matcher instance
gotchas_discovered.impact: Critical for performance in fast typing; avoids repeated allocation/deallocation

gotchas_discovered.context: When query is empty, all cards should match with no highlights
gotchas_discovered.solution: Early return with empty title_indices when self.query.is_empty()
gotchas_discovered.impact: Prevents unnecessary pattern matching and nucleo overhead when no filter is active

gotchas_discovered.issue: Filter state persistence across mode transitions
gotchas_discovered.context: User enters filter mode, types query, presses Esc (should clear), presses / again (should start fresh)
gotchas_discovered.solution: clear_filter() drops filter_state entirely; enter_filter_mode() checks self.filter to seed new state
gotchas_discovered.impact: If filter_state was not dropped, re-entering filter mode would restore previous query instead of starting fresh

approach_details.implementation_strategy: Modular filter state abstraction (FilterState struct) encapsulates nucleo matcher and query; integrated into app lifecycle via Mode::Filter; input handler wires keystroke to filter operations; kanban renderer applies highlight indices to card title spans
key_decisions: Separated FilterMatch (result) from FilterState (mutable state) for clean data flow
Used Option<FilterState> to denote active filter mode vs Option<String> for persisted filter query
Applied filter collapse on every keystroke to provide real-time feedback
Combined haystack approach balances search flexibility against complexity
integration_points: app.rs: Mode::Filter state machine, filter persistence
kanban.rs: highlight rendering using match indices
Footer (not shown in diff): displays 'Filter: {query}█' prompt with instructions
approach_details.test_coverage: 6 unit tests in filter.rs covering: empty query, title match, ID match, no match, sorted indices, case insensitivity

recommendations.suggestion: Memoize FilterMatch results per card to avoid recalculating matches on every render frame
recommendations.rationale: With large card counts (100+), recalculating all matches every frame (60 FPS) becomes expensive; cache matches and only recalculate when query changes

recommendations.suggestion: Add filter history (recent queries) accessible via up/down arrows in filter mode
recommendations.rationale: Users often apply similar filters repeatedly; history reduces retyping and improves workflow

recommendations.suggestion: Add filter preview count to footer (e.g., 'Filter: feat  [3 cards]  [Esc]clear')
recommendations.rationale: Users need visibility into how many cards match current query; provides confidence before confirming

recommendations.suggestion: Consider extracting haystack construction into a dedicated method for reusability and testing
recommendations.rationale: If future filtering modes (by provider, by state) are added, a shared haystack builder reduces duplication

recommendations.suggestion: Add integration test in app.rs verifying filter mode state transitions and column collapse behavior
recommendations.rationale: Current tests are unit-level; integration test ensures Mode::Filter → confirm → Normal → re-enter works correctly

recommendations.suggestion: Add example in filter.rs docstring showing typical usage flow (enter → type → confirm → clear)
recommendations.rationale: Future maintainers can understand filter lifecycle without reading input.rs and app.rs
